title: Learn Fares lesson by lesson.
description: Study fare topics in focused lessons, take a mini quiz, review wrong answers, and finish with a mixed Fares test using every loaded original question.

lessons.title: Fare Basics
lessons.intro: Start with the core ideas: when a fare begins, what the passenger can expect, and how a fare should be handled clearly.
lessons.memoryTrick: Before fare detail, remember the three Cs: confirm, charge correctly, communicate.
commonMistakes: Mixing general fare duties with extra-charge rules
Forgetting receipt and passenger communication basics

lessons.title: Standard, Premium and Special Rates
lessons.intro: Learn how rate types differ so you can recognise the correct charging context quickly.
lessons.memoryTrick: Standard is the baseline, premium is time-sensitive, special is event or rule-specific.
commonMistakes: Treating premium and special rates as the same thing
Missing the condition that triggers a rate

lessons.title: Tariff A and Tariff B
lessons.intro: Focus on the difference between Tariff A and Tariff B and when each one applies.
lessons.memoryTrick: A comes first; B begins after the Tariff A threshold.
commonMistakes: Choosing the tariff from habit instead of the question facts
Ignoring time, date, or rate clues

lessons.title: Time vs Distance Charging
lessons.intro: Understand when the fare is affected by time, distance, traffic, or waiting.
lessons.memoryTrick: Fast means distance; slow traffic means time.
commonMistakes: Assuming only distance changes the fare

lessons.title: Extra Charges
lessons.intro: Practise recognising valid extra charges and spotting charges that should not be added.
lessons.memoryTrick: Extras need a rule. If no rule supports it, do not add it.
commonMistakes: Adding extras because a trip feels inconvenient
Confusing luggage, passenger, booking, toll, or soiling charges

lessons.title: Taximeter Rules
lessons.intro: Review how the taximeter should be used, displayed, and relied on during a journey.
lessons.memoryTrick: Meter visible, meter correct, meter used.
commonMistakes: Starting the meter at the wrong moment

lessons.title: Fixed Payment Offences
lessons.intro: Learn the offence patterns that lead to fixed payment notices.
lessons.memoryTrick: Penalty questions usually turn on one key action: fail, refuse, display, operate, or comply.
commonMistakes: Reading offence questions as general customer-service questions

lessons.title: Customer Service and Driver Rights
lessons.intro: Balance passenger service expectations with the rights and responsibilities of a professional driver.
lessons.memoryTrick: Be fair, be clear, be professional.
commonMistakes: Assuming drivers must accept every situation
Forgetting passenger-facing duties such as receipts

lessons.title: Final Mixed Fares Test
lessons.intro: Take every Fares question together to check whether you can handle mixed wording without lesson hints.
lessons.memoryTrick: Read facts first, identify topic second, choose rule last.
commonMistakes: Answering by lesson title instead of question facts
Rushing rate, tariff, and extra-charge questions

questions.questionText: When should a taxi driver be prepared to provide a receipt?
questions.explanation: A professional SPSV service should support receipts when requested by the passenger.
questions.memoryTip: Receipt questions usually turn on the passenger request.
questions.commonMistake: Do not limit receipt duties to a payment method, journey type, or fare amount unless the original question says so.
